<Meta title="Molecules/Meta/Profile Meta" />

# Profile Meta

Displays professional metadata for person profiles, including titles, departments, and pronouns, with an optional portrait or landscape image.

## Interactive Preview

<Canvas of={MetaStories.Profile} />
<Controls of={MetaStories.Profile} />

## When to use it

Use Profile Meta on faculty profiles, staff directory entries, and leadership pages. It renders the structured header region for a person node — name, title, department, and pronouns — with an optional image at a configurable orientation and alignment.

## Image Options

The **Profile Image Orientation** control switches between `landscape` (3x2) and `portrait` (2x3) crops. The **Profile Image Alignment** control places the image to the `left` or `right` of the text block.

Use the **Profile Image Style** control to choose between `inline` (image shares the header row) and other available layout styles.

## Pronouns

Include pronouns when available. Pronouns are rendered with appropriate semantic markup and displayed alongside the name to support inclusive communication.

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Molecule
- **Template**: `yds-profile-meta.twig`
- **Styles**: `_yds-profile-meta.scss`

### Accessibility

- Pronouns rendered with semantic markup for screen reader clarity
- Image alt text required when an image is provided
- Proper heading hierarchy within the profile layout
- Color contrast meets WCAG AA standards across all background color options

## Related Components

- **Image**: Used for the optional profile image
- **Post Meta**: Use for editorial content attribution instead of Profile Meta
